programming_language: Next.js, React, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI, Framer Motion, Anime.js
original_requirements: Create a multiplayer, real-time web app version of the physical card game 'Bunny Warren' using Next.js, Tailwind CSS 4, Radix UI, Framer Motion, and Anime.js.
product_goals: Provide an engaging and interactive multiplayer experience for players.
Ensure smooth and real-time gameplay with minimal latency.
Maintain the core mechanics and fun of the original card game.
user_stories: As a player, I want to join a game room so that I can play with friends.
As a player, I want to see real-time updates of the game state so that I can make informed moves.
As a player, I want to chat with other players during the game so that we can strategize and socialize.
As a player, I want to customize my game settings so that I can tailor the game to my preferences.
As a player, I want to view game statistics so that I can track my performance over time.

competitive_analysis.pros: Wide variety of games, strong community, cross-platform support.
competitive_analysis.cons: Complex interface, requires subscription for full features.

competitive_analysis.pros: Realistic 3D graphics, extensive game library, free to play.
competitive_analysis.cons: High system requirements, limited social features.

competitive_analysis.pros: Turn-based gameplay, no downloads required, free to play.
Real-time and turn-based options, strong community, easy to use.
Highly customizable, supports modding, realistic physics.

requirements_analysis: The web app needs to support real-time multiplayer gameplay, including game state synchronization, player interactions, and chat functionality. It should also provide a user-friendly interface for game setup and customization. The app must be responsive and optimized for both desktop and mobile devices.

requirements_pool.P1: Multiplayer game room creation and joining

ui_design_draft: The UI should feature a clean and intuitive design with a game lobby, game room, and in-game interface. The game lobby will allow players to create or join game rooms, while the game room will display the current game state, player actions, and chat. The in-game interface will include the game board, player hands, and action buttons.
open_questions: How will we handle game state synchronization across different devices?
What are the specific customization options we want to offer to players?
How will we ensure the security and privacy of user data?
